heading: Art. 112. Drunkenness and other incapacitation offenses
label: 912 Art. 112. Drunkenness and other incapacitation offenses
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)91250:706.(Art. 112), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 139

Amendments2016— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any person subject to this chapter other than a sentinel or look-out, who is found drunk on duty, shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.”Pub. L. 114–328

Effective Date of 2016 AmendmentAmendment by effective on , as designated by the President, with implementing regulations and provisions relating to applicability to various situations, see and Ex. Ord. No. 13825, set out as notes under .Pub. L. 114–328section 5542 of Pub. L. 114–328section 801 of this titleJan. 1, 2019

children.heading: .—Drunk on Duty
children.label: a .—Drunk on Duty
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who is drunk on duty shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.

children.heading: .—Incapacitation for Duty From Drunkenness or Drug Use
children.label: b .—Incapacitation for Duty From Drunkenness or Drug Use
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who, as a result of indulgence in any alcoholic beverage or any drug, is incapacitated for the proper performance of duty shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.

children.heading: .—Drunk Prisoner
children.label: c .—Drunk Prisoner
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who is a prisoner and, while in such status, is drunk shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
